heading: Community participation
label: 3203 Community participation
notes: Effective DateSection effective on the first day of the first full program year after (), see , set out as a note under .July 22, 2014July 1, 2015section 506 of Pub. L. 113–128section 3101 of this title

children.heading: Business and community participation
children.label: a Business and community participation
children.text: The director of each Job Corps center shall ensure the establishment and development of the mutually beneficial business and community relationships and networks described in subsection (b), including the use of local boards, in order to enhance the effectiveness of such centers.

children.label: A
children.text: local and distant employers, to the extent practicable, in coordination with entities carrying out other Federal and non-Federal programs that conduct similar outreach to employers;

children.label: i
children.text: information to, and referral of, potential enrollees; and

children.label: ii
children.text: job opportunities for Job Corps graduates; and

children.label: i
children.text: entities carrying out relevant apprenticeship programs and youth programs;

children.label: ii
children.text: labor-management organizations and local labor organizations;

children.label: iii
children.text: employers and contractors that support national training contractor programs; and

children.label: iv
children.text: community-based organizations, non-profit organizations, and intermediaries providing workforce development-related services; and

establishing and developing relationships with members of the community in which the Job Corps center is located, informing members of the community about the projects of the Job Corps center and changes in the rules, procedures, or activities of the center that may affect the community, and planning events of mutual interest to the community and the Job Corps center.

children.heading: New centers
children.label: c New centers
children.text: The director of a Job Corps center that is not yet operating shall ensure the establishment and development of the relationships and networks described in subsection (b) at least 3 months prior to the date on which the center accepts the first enrollee at the center.
